core_identity.title: Standard Form: From Galaxies to Cells
core_identity.author: Gerry Docherty

curriculum_alignment.subject: mathematics
curriculum_alignment.subtopic: scientific notation and calculations using powers of 10
curriculum_alignment.seeds_of_change: Write very large and very small numbers in standard form a × 10^n with 1 ≤ |a| < 10, convert between ordinary and standard form reliably, and perform calculations (×, ÷, +, −) in standard form while interpreting scale across science contexts (space to cells). :contentReference[oaicite:0]{index=0}

verse_ality.driving_question: How can standard form help us make sense of the biggest and smallest measurements in the universe?

knowing_world.focus: Standard form (scientific notation) expresses very large or very small numbers concisely using powers of 10. Learners practise converting, and applying operation rules for multiplication, division, and addition/subtraction while keeping the coefficient a between 1 and 10.
key_concepts: Converting by moving the decimal point and counting places
Multiplying in standard form: multiply a parts, add exponents
Dividing in standard form: divide a parts, subtract exponents
Adding/subtracting in standard form: match exponents before combining
Renormalising to keep a in range (adjusting a and n)
activities: Multiply 3.2 × 10^5 by 4.5 × 10^3 and express the result in standard form.
Add 7.5 × 10^4 + 2.3 × 10^3 and express the result in standard form.

human_story.focus: Scientists and engineers use standard form daily to work with astronomical distances, microscopic sizes, engineering tolerances, and data scales. Standard form can support clearer communication and decision-making where access to advanced tools is limited.
justice_prompts: In places with limited computing tools, people may struggle to interpret large/small numbers. How could standard form democratize access to scientific data and equitable decision-making (e.g., climate science, medicine)?

expressive_self.focus: Learners connect scale to imagination by representing space- and cell-scale measurements side-by-side, and by explaining standard form as a ‘power of ten’ language.
activities: Sketch two scenes side by side: solar-system distances and cell organelles; label key measurements using standard form.
Write a short metaphor/poem about powers of ten (e.g., from galaxy to atom).
Record a short voice note explaining why standard form is ‘superpower notation’.

designed_world.focus: Use an interactive matching tool to connect real-world measurements (planets to particles) with their standard form equivalents, then extend to calculation and comparison tasks.
tools: Canva embed: real-life measurement to standard form matching tool
activities: Match 149,600,000 km (Earth → Sun) to its standard form equivalent.
Match 0.000012 m (cell organelle diameter) to its standard form equivalent.
Multiply 2.5 × 10^6 by 4 × 10^3 and convert the result to standard form.
Add 5.0 × 10^4 + 2.3 × 10^5 and give the answer in standard form.
Compare which is larger: 3.0 × 10^-4 vs 2.0 × 10^-3, and explain in words.
design_challenges: Create a mini ‘scale set’ of 10 measurements (mix of space and cell scale), convert each into standard form, and write a one-line justification for the exponent choice.

regulation_supports: Stretch arms wide for 5 seconds, relax.
Eye reset: look far (about 20 m) for 20 seconds, then near focus for 20 seconds.

pathways.name: Final Task - Power of Ten Portfolio
pathways.focus: Create a two-page portfolio demonstrating conversions, calculations, and interpretation of scale using standard form.
products: Page 1: Five real-world measurements (space and/or cell scale) with standard form equivalents
Page 2: Five random numbers (large and small) converted to/from standard form with working
Short reflection: when standard form feels easiest/trickiest and why
assessment_criteria: Conceptual Understanding — Explains why exponents move decimals; interprets positive/negative exponents
Fluency & Accuracy — Correct conversions, consistent notation, final answers with 1 ≤ |a| < 10
Application — Uses real-life scale data and justifies exponent choices
Independence & Reflection — Identifies an error to avoid next time and a strategy to prevent it

required: Basic index/power awareness (10^n meaning repeated factors of 10)

builds_toward: Calculations with standard form in science contexts
